signs-your-parent-needs-home-care: Deciding whether an aging parent needs help at home is rarely simple. Changes often happen gradually, and many older adults are reluctant to ask for support. Recognizing the early signs can help you act before a small problem becomes a crisis. Here are ten signs that it may be time to consider in-home care.
Changes in personal hygiene and appearance
If your parent is wearing the same clothes for days, skipping showers, or struggling with grooming, it may signal that bathing and dressing have become difficult or unsafe to manage alone.
A messier or unsafe home
Piling mail, unwashed dishes, spoiled food in the fridge, or clutter in walkways can point to difficulty keeping up with daily tasks, and growing fall risks.
Forgetfulness with medications
Missed doses, double doses, or expired prescriptions are common and serious. A caregiver can provide medication reminders and help keep routines on track.
Recent falls or unsteadiness
Bruises, a new fear of falling, or holding onto furniture to move around the house are red flags. Mobility support and a safer home environment can make a real difference.
Weight loss or poor nutrition
Empty cupboards, reliance on snacks, or noticeable weight loss may mean cooking and shopping have become too much. Meal planning and preparation can restore healthy eating.
Increased isolation or low mood
Withdrawing from friends, hobbies, or family can affect both emotional and physical health. Companion care brings conversation, activity, and connection back into the day.
Trouble managing finances
Unpaid bills, unusual purchases, or confusion over money can be early signs that more day-to-day support is needed.
Difficulty with daily activities
Struggling with cooking, laundry, or getting around the house, the activities of daily living, is one of the clearest signs that help would improve quality of life.
Caregiver burnout in the family
If you or other family members are exhausted from providing care, respite care offers professional support so you can rest and recharge while your loved one is in good hands.
Missed appointments or worsening health
Forgetting doctor visits or a chronic condition that's getting harder to manage may call for skilled nursing support at home.
What to do next

If several of these signs sound familiar, you're not alone, and you don't have to figure it out by yourself. At My Home Cares, we offer free, no-obligation consultations across Maryland to help you understand your options and build a care plan that fits. Call (410) 231-3076 or schedule a consultation to talk it through.
how-to-choose-home-care-agency-maryland: Choosing a home care agency for someone you love is a big decision. The right agency brings peace of mind; the wrong one brings stress. In Maryland, home care is regulated, which gives families a helpful starting point for evaluating providers. Use this checklist to compare agencies with confidence.
Confirm the agency is a licensed Maryland RSA

In Maryland, agencies that provide home care are licensed as Residential Service Agencies (RSAs) and overseen by the Office of Health Care Quality (OHCQ). Always ask for the agency's RSA license number. My Home Cares is licensed under RSA-01229 | HCSA-00845.
Ask how caregivers are screened and trained

Reputable agencies run criminal background checks, verify certifications, and train caregivers to state standards. Ask whether caregivers are employees (covered by insurance and supervision) rather than contractors, and how the agency handles training and ongoing oversight.
Every person's needs are different. A good agency completes an assessment and builds an individualized care plan, then updates it as needs change, rather than offering a one-size-fits-all service.
Needs evolve over time. Choosing an agency that offers skilled nursing, personal care, companion care, respite, and homemaking means you won't have to switch providers as circumstances change.
Ask for clear pricing and which payment methods are accepted, private pay, long-term care insurance, and Medicaid waiver programs. A trustworthy agency will explain your options without pressure.

How quickly does the agency answer the phone and respond to questions? Reliable communication is one of the best predictors of reliable care. Ask how they handle scheduling, caregiver changes, and emergencies.
Read reviews and ask for references
Online reviews and word-of-mouth from other families offer real insight. Don't hesitate to ask the agency for references.
Questions to ask on your first call

What is your RSA license number? How do you screen and train caregivers? Will my loved one have consistent caregivers? How do you create and update the care plan? What happens if a caregiver is sick? How soon can care begin?

A great home care agency is licensed, transparent, responsive, and genuinely person-centered. If you'd like to see how My Home Cares measures up, schedule a free consultation, we're happy to answer every question on this list. Call (410) 231-3076.
how-to-pay-for-home-care-maryland: One of the first questions Maryland families ask about home care is, "How will we pay for it?" The good news is there are several options, and many families use a combination of them. Here's a plain-English overview to help you plan.

Many families begin with private pay, using personal income, savings, or family contributions. It offers the most flexibility in choosing services and schedules. Agencies should provide clear, upfront pricing so you can budget with confidence.

If your loved one has a long-term care insurance policy, it may cover in-home care. Review the policy's benefit amount, elimination (waiting) period, and what services qualify. A good agency can help you understand what documentation the insurer needs and bill accordingly.

For those who qualify financially, Maryland Medicaid offers home- and community-based programs designed to help people receive care at home instead of in a facility, for example, Community First Choice (CFC) and the Community Options waiver. Eligibility and covered services change over time, so it's best to confirm current programs and requirements with Maryland Medicaid or ask our team to point you in the right direction.
Families often blend these options, for instance, starting with private pay while a long-term care insurance claim or Medicaid application is processed. Planning ahead helps avoid gaps in care.
How My Home Cares can help

We know navigating payment can feel overwhelming. During a free consultation, we'll talk through your situation, explain which options may apply, and help you build an affordable care plan. Call (410) 231-3076 or schedule a consultation to get started.
This article is general information, not financial or legal advice. Program details and eligibility can change, please confirm current specifics with the relevant program or a qualified advisor.
world-diabetes-day-raising-awareness: World Diabetes Day: Raising Awareness and Supporting Health with My Home Cares

November 14 marks World Diabetes Day, a day dedicated to raising awareness about diabetes and promoting better health for those affected by this condition. Diabetes can bring daily challenges in managing blood sugar levels, maintaining a healthy lifestyle, and preventing complications. At My Home Cares, we are committed to providing compassionate support to individuals managing diabetes, helping them live healthier, more comfortable lives at home.

Diabetes is a chronic condition that affects millions of people worldwide. It requires careful, consistent management to prevent complications such as cardiovascular issues, neuropathy, and vision problems. For those managing diabetes, home care can play a vital role in supporting healthy routines, from monitoring blood glucose levels to maintaining a balanced diet.
How My Home Cares Supports Clients with Diabetes

At My Home Cares, our skilled caregivers provide essential support for individuals living with diabetes. Our team assists with tasks like meal planning, medication reminders, and blood sugar monitoring to help clients stay on track with their health goals. Additionally, we offer education and guidance to empower clients in managing their condition confidently and independently.

Education is a powerful tool in diabetes management. On World Diabetes Day, we encourage everyone to learn more about diabetes, its risk factors, and preventive measures. By making small lifestyle adjustments, such as regular exercise and mindful eating, many people can reduce their risk of type 2 diabetes.
Join Us in Supporting Diabetes Awareness

This World Diabetes Day, let’s stand together to support those affected by diabetes and to promote awareness in our communities. At My Home Cares, we’re dedicated to empowering individuals to manage their health with confidence and compassion.
Together, let’s build a healthier future!
celebrating-world-kindness-day: Celebrating World Kindness Day: Spreading Compassion with My Home Cares

World Kindness Day, observed every year on November 13, is a celebration of the positive impact kindness has on individuals and communities. At My Home Cares, we believe that kindness is essential to compassionate caregiving. Whether through a smile, a comforting word, or an act of support, our caregivers strive to bring warmth and empathy to every interaction.
The Power of Kindness in Caregiving

Kindness is at the heart of quality care. It builds trust, reduces stress, and enhances emotional well-being for both clients and caregivers. In home care, small gestures—like patiently listening or offering a reassuring touch—can make a world of difference. Our mission is to foster a compassionate environment where clients feel valued and supported.

Kindness doesn’t need to be grand. On World Kindness Day, we encourage everyone to take a moment to show appreciation, lend a hand, or simply brighten someone’s day. Acts like helping a neighbor, volunteering, or sharing a meal can create ripples of positivity that strengthen communities.
Join Us in Celebrating Kindness Every Day

At My Home Cares, kindness is more than a single day; it’s a way of life. This World Kindness Day, let’s all commit to making kindness a daily practice. Together, we can build a more compassionate world, one kind act at a time.
Happy World Kindness Day from My Home Cares!
maryland-medicaid-renewal-check-your-mai: If you or a loved one has Maryland Medicaid, one small habit can protect your coverage: check your mail. Maryland reviews every enrollee's eligibility once a year, and the notice that starts that process arrives by mail, or in your online account if you've gone paperless. Miss it, and you could lose coverage you still qualify for, coverage that may even be paying for care at home.
Why this matters for home care

Many of the families we serve rely on Maryland Medicaid home- and community-based waiver programs, such as Community First Choice and the Community Options Waiver, to help pay for care at home. If Medicaid coverage lapses because a renewal was missed, that funding can be interrupted, even when the person still meets every requirement. A few minutes of attention to the mail can prevent a stressful, avoidable gap in care.
How Maryland Medicaid renewal works

Maryland Medicaid coverage is renewed every 12 months. You may hear it called redetermination, recertification, or reapplying. When it's your turn, the state checks that you still meet the income requirements (and, for some programs, the asset requirements) for your coverage.

You'll receive a notice, by mail or in your online account, when it's time to renew, and you then have 60 days to respond. In some cases the state can renew you automatically using information it already has. If that happens, you'll still get a notice letting you know there's nothing more for you to do.
Watch the mail, and don't toss official envelopes

Renewal notices come from the Maryland Department of Health, Maryland Health Connection, or your local Department of Social Services. It's easy to mistake these for junk mail and set them aside. If you see an envelope from any of them, open it right away and note any deadline printed inside.
Keep your address and phone number current

The single most common reason people lose Medicaid is a renewal notice sent to an old address. A lot can change in a year. If you've moved, changed your phone number, or changed your email, update your information now, before your renewal comes due, so the notice actually reaches you.

Most enrollees update their information and renew through Maryland Health Connection, online at marylandhealthconnection.gov or by phone at 1-855-642-8572. If you are age 65 or older, blind or disabled, or enrolled in a Home and Community-Based Services waiver program, you renew through the Maryland Department of Human Services instead, online at marylandbenefits.gov or by phone at 1-800-332-6347. You can also update your details or renew in person at your local Department of Social Services.
Report changes within 10 days

Apart from your yearly renewal, report any change in your income or household within 10 days. That includes moving, a change in income, a change in who lives with you, or a change in disability status. Reporting promptly keeps your record accurate and helps prevent an unexpected loss of coverage.
Lean on your support team

If you receive Medicaid waiver services, you don't have to manage renewals on your own. We strongly encourage participants to work closely with their support planning agency representative and their residential service agency (RSA) representative. These are the people who help coordinate your care, and they can help you keep track of renewal dates, make sense of any notice you receive, and make sure the right paperwork is completed and submitted on time.
If you're no longer eligible

If a renewal finds that you no longer qualify for Medicaid, you still have options. Low-cost health plans are available through Maryland Health Connection, and you can enroll within 60 days of losing your coverage. And if you were using Medicaid to help pay for home care, talk with us, we can discuss other ways to keep care in place while you sort out your coverage.
A quick checklist

Check your mail and your online account regularly. Confirm your address, phone, and email are current. Open anything from Maryland Medicaid, Maryland Health Connection, or Social Services the day it arrives. Respond to a renewal notice within 60 days. Report income or household changes within 10 days. If you're a waiver participant, stay in close touch with your support planning agency and residential service agency representatives. And when in doubt, call rather than wait.
We're here to help

We're a home care agency, not a Medicaid office, so we can't make eligibility decisions for you. But we help Maryland families navigate these steps all the time, and we're glad to point you in the right direction. If you have questions about paying for home care or keeping coverage in place, call us at (410) 231-3076 or reach out for a free consultation.

This article is general information, not legal or benefits advice. For guidance on your specific situation, contact Maryland Health Connection at 1-855-642-8572, the Maryland Department of Human Services at 1-800-332-6347, or your local Department of Social Services.
